issues.body: The page says every PARTIAL/GAP row that names future work links to its governing bead, and this file repeats that as an honesty contract. However, multiple rows describe future work without `beads`, including "Query-time rewriting mode + multiple named schemas" (PARTIAL), "SWRL ingestion" (GAP), and "Ontology / shape editor" (GAP). The latter two even say they are not beaded. Users therefore do not receive the promised build-in-public tracking links, so the page's central completeness claim is false.
issues.fix_hint: Either create/provide governing tracker beads for every PARTIAL/GAP row that names future work and populate `beads`, or narrow the comments and rendered copy to accurately state that only tracked work links to beads. Add a data-level test that enforces whichever invariant is chosen so future rows cannot silently contradict the copy.
issues.title: Future-work rows violate the advertised bead-link completeness invariant

verdict.summary: The comparison page is structurally straightforward and its ZK/MPC language is appropriately caveated, but its central build-in-public completeness claim is contradicted by the matrix data. Several PARTIAL/GAP rows describe future work while providing no bead link, even though both the page and data-layer honesty contract promise that every such row links its governing tracker item. This makes a prominent user-facing claim false and should be corrected before approval.
